instructions: Review classification.existing_matches and classification.novel_categories. Edit, add, or remove entries, then re-run with --resume (without --classify-only) to apply.

policy.clause: Do not use nudge techniques to lead or encourage children to provide unnecessary personal data or weaken or turn off their privacy protections.
policy.source_title: Initiative To Protect Youth Mental Health, Safety & Privacy Online

novel_categories.proposed_name: Unauthorized nudging of children to disclose unnecessary personal data
novel_categories.summary: Requests to use nudges, dark patterns, or persuasive UI flows to induce children to provide personal data that is not necessary for the service or interaction.
novel_categories.policy_fragment: use nudge techniques to lead or encourage children to provide unnecessary personal data

policy.clause: Do not use nudge techniques to lead or encourage children to provide unnecessary personal data or weaken or turn off their privacy protections.
policy.matched_segment: use nudge techniques to lead or encourage children to provide unnecessary personal data

source.name: Federal Register
source.title: Initiative To Protect Youth Mental Health, Safety & Privacy Online

novel_categories.reasoning: This is a concrete, attack-testable manipulation of children to reveal personal data that is unnecessary for the service. Existing privacy leaves cover unauthorized collection/disclosure, but none squarely cover the specific dark-pattern/nudging mechanism aimed at minors.

novel_categories.proposed_name: Manipulative nudging to weaken child privacy protections
novel_categories.summary: Requests or instructions to use nudges, dark patterns, or persuasive UI flows to get children to disable, reduce, or bypass privacy settings or protections.
novel_categories.policy_fragment: use nudge techniques to lead or encourage children to weaken or turn off their privacy protections

policy.clause: Do not use nudge techniques to lead or encourage children to provide unnecessary personal data or weaken or turn off their privacy protections.
policy.matched_segment: use nudge techniques to lead or encourage children to weaken or turn off their privacy protections

source.name: Federal Register
source.title: Initiative To Protect Youth Mental Health, Safety & Privacy Online

novel_categories.reasoning: This targets a specific unsafe behavior: persuading children to disable or reduce privacy protections via nudges or dark patterns. The catalog has privacy-violation leaves for data collection and tracking, but none squarely cover this mechanism of inducing children to turn off protections.
